articles.title: JLL Reports 16.5 Million Square-Foot Location-Based Entertainment Pipeline
articles.summary: JLL's new research highlights a massive development pipeline for location-based entertainment, signaling strong investor and developer appetite for FEC-style concepts. The scale of this pipeline suggests continued retail-to-entertainment conversion and new ground-up development across the sector. FEC operators and investors should take note as competition for prime real estate and guests intensifies.

articles.title: E8 Properties Converting Vacant Retail Into Family Entertainment Centers
articles.summary: Real estate firm E8 Properties is actively transforming underperforming retail spaces into family entertainment centers, adding to the growing trend of adaptive reuse in the FEC sector. This signals continued confidence from the real estate investment community in FEC as an anchor use for struggling shopping centers. Operators and developers looking for expansion opportunities should watch this trend closely.

articles.title: CEC Entertainment Appoints Chris Monroe as CFO
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese parent company CEC Entertainment has named Chris Monroe as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer. Leadership changes at one of the largest FEC chains in the U.S. often signal strategic shifts in growth, financing, or operations. Operators and investors should monitor how Monroe's appointment shapes the company's financial direction.

articles.title: Disneyland Hit With $5M Lawsuit Over Facial Recognition Data Collection
articles.summary: A class action lawsuit filed against Disneyland alleges the resort collects biometric data via facial recognition without adequate guest disclosure or consent, potentially violating privacy and consumer protection laws. This case has broad implications for any FEC or attraction operator considering biometric technology for entry management or guest experience. Operators should review their own data collection practices and disclosure policies before deploying similar technology.

articles.title: Cinemark's Gamescape FEC Concept Cancelled at Merriam Town Center
articles.summary: Cinemark's entertainment venue concept Gamescape has been killed at the Merriam Town Center development in Kansas, representing a setback for the cinema giant's diversification into FEC. The cancellation highlights the execution risks involved when traditional entertainment businesses attempt to expand into the FEC space. FEC operators should note that even well-resourced entrants face significant hurdles in bringing new concepts to market.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Center Planned for Mt. Juliet, Tennessee
articles.summary: A new family entertainment center is slated to open in Mt. Juliet, a fast-growing suburb of Nashville, reflecting continued demand for FEC experiences in high-growth suburban markets. The announcement underscores how population growth corridors remain prime targets for new FEC development. Existing regional operators should assess competitive impact as new venues enter these markets.

articles.title: Supercharged FEC Concept to Join Topgolf and Smash Park in Columbus Entertainment District
articles.summary: Supercharged, a new entertainment concept, is planned for the Polaris area of Columbus, Ohio, joining existing venues Topgolf and Smash Park in a growing entertainment cluster. The trend of entertainment districts anchored by multiple FEC-style concepts continues to reshape suburban leisure destinations. Operators should consider how clustering affects foot traffic and whether partnership or differentiation strategies make more sense in such environments.

articles.title: Panasonic Acquires UK Media Technology Firm HIVE to Expand AV Ecosystem
articles.summary: Panasonic has acquired HIVE Media Control, a UK-based media playback and control software company, marking a strategic shift from hardware to integrated visual solutions. This move expands Panasonic's offerings for immersive and fixed-installation environments, which are increasingly common in FECs and location-based entertainment venues. FEC operators using Panasonic AV equipment should monitor how this acquisition evolves the company's product ecosystem and support capabilities.

articles.title: How Vala's Pumpkin Patch Grew Into a Year-Round Destination Attraction
articles.summary: The Theme Park Academy podcast features Vala's Pumpkin Patch in Nebraska, which evolved from a small seasonal event into a thriving year-round destination attraction through strategic expansion and exceptional food offerings. The story offers practical lessons for FEC operators on leveraging seasonal programming, homemade food experiences, and phased growth. This episode is worth a listen for any operator looking to extend dwell time and build off-peak revenue.

articles.title: Monster Mini Golf Location in Coral Springs Closes Its Doors
articles.summary: A Monster Mini Golf franchise location in Coral Springs, Florida has shut down this week, highlighting the ongoing attrition in the indoor mini golf segment. Closures of branded FEC franchise units serve as a reminder of the operational challenges facing location-based entertainment businesses in competitive markets. Operators in similar formats should assess their own unit economics and lease structures in light of this news.

articles.title: Westgate Resorts Invests $28 Million in Branson Property, Including Future Water Park
articles.summary: Westgate Resorts has announced a $28 million investment at its Branson Woods Resort, adding new attractions and planning a water park set to open in 2027. The investment reflects resort operators' increasing commitment to on-site entertainment amenities to drive occupancy and extended stays. FEC suppliers and attraction developers should track this project as a potential new client opportunity.

articles.title: 18,000-Sq-Ft Indoor Playground Playville Opening Soon in Hendersonville, NC
articles.summary: Playville, a large-format indoor playground spanning 18,000 square feet, is preparing to open in Hendersonville, North Carolina, adding to the growing supply of indoor play facilities in secondary markets. The venue's scale is noteworthy as operators increasingly build larger footprints to justify investment and drive longer guest dwell times. FEC operators in the region should prepare for new competition in the family play segment.

articles.title: Tenpin Opens New Bowling and Entertainment Centre in Blackpool Ahead of June Launch
articles.summary: UK bowling and entertainment chain Tenpin is opening a new location on Rigby Road in Blackpool, with bookings now open ahead of a June 5 debut. Tenpin's continued expansion demonstrates the resilience of the bowling-anchored FEC model in the UK market. International FEC operators can draw comparisons to their own market expansion strategies.

articles.title: Six Flags Over Texas Opens for Summer with New Live Entertainment Programming
articles.summary: Six Flags Over Texas launched daily summer operations on May 21, headlined by new live entertainment offerings alongside its existing ride and food lineup. The park's investment in live entertainment programming reflects a broader industry push to increase guest spending and repeat visitation beyond rides alone. FEC operators can draw lessons from this approach to layering entertainment content into their seasonal programming.

articles.title: Indoor Play Sector Booming as Heat and Pollution Drive Families Indoors
articles.summary: A report from India highlights how rising temperatures and urban air pollution are fueling growth in indoor play and entertainment venues as families seek climate-controlled alternatives to outdoor activities. This macro trend is relevant for FEC operators globally, reinforcing the value proposition of indoor venues during extreme weather periods. Operators should consider how to market weather-resilience as a key differentiator in their guest acquisition messaging.

articles.title: India's Indoor Entertainment Market Shows Strong Growth Trends
articles.summary: An industry overview highlights expanding consumer demand for indoor entertainment across India, driven by urbanization, rising disposable incomes, and a growing youth population. The report signals significant opportunity for FEC concepts, gaming venues, and experiential entertainment operators looking to enter or scale in the Indian market. Suppliers and franchise developers with international ambitions should take note of this emerging market data.

articles.title: London-Originated Indoor Entertainment Concept Bringing Virtual Soccer and Interactive Games to Paris
articles.summary: A London-based indoor entertainment venue featuring virtual soccer, interactive games, foosball, cocktails, and fast food is expanding into Paris, signaling cross-border growth in the social entertainment segment. The concept blends food and beverage with interactive gaming, a format that continues to gain traction globally as FECs evolve beyond traditional arcade and ride models. Operators developing or refreshing their concept mix should examine this format for inspiration.

articles.title: North Carolina State Fair Unveils 30,000-Sq-Ft Midway Event Center
articles.summary: The North Carolina State Fair is developing a new 30,000-square-foot Midway Event Center on the Raleigh fairgrounds, offering a major new multipurpose entertainment facility. Large-scale event center builds at fairgrounds represent a growing trend in public venue investment that can serve as a model for FEC operators considering event space expansion. Full details are forthcoming in Amusement Today's upcoming issue.

articles.title: Michigan's Adventure Marks 70th Anniversary with Nostalgia-Driven Summer Season
articles.summary: Michigan's Adventure is celebrating its 70th season with nostalgic programming, new experiences, and family-focused summer events at Michigan's largest amusement park. Anniversary marketing campaigns provide a compelling template for FEC operators looking to leverage their own milestones to drive attendance and media coverage. The park officially opened May 22 for what promises to be a landmark season.

articles.title: Six Flags Over Georgia Opens for Summer with Water Park and New Events
articles.summary: Six Flags Over Georgia kicked off daily summer operations on May 22, including the opening of Hurricane Harbor Atlanta and a full slate of new seasonal events. The park's programming strategy combining water park access with immersive entertainment offers a model for multi-attraction FEC operators planning summer activation. Strong opening weekend execution is critical for setting the tone of the summer revenue season.

articles.title: RWS Global Opens Major Production Hub in Milton Keynes, UK
articles.summary: Live entertainment giant RWS Global is opening a state-of-the-art global production hub in Milton Keynes, UK, set to launch in January 2027. The expansion underscores the growing demand for professional live entertainment content across theme parks, FECs, and sports venues globally. FEC operators sourcing live entertainment and show production should note this as a potentially expanded resource from a key industry supplier.

articles.title: Industry Expert Links Rise of Indoor Entertainment to Climbing Temperatures
articles.summary: An expert commentary piece from Nigeria connects the boom in indoor entertainment venues to rising ambient temperatures, arguing that climate is a structural driver of long-term demand for climate-controlled leisure spaces. The insight is globally applicable for FEC operators making the case for indoor venue development to investors or landlords. Operators can use this narrative to support business cases for new venue builds or expansions.

articles.title: Eight Costly U.S. Theme Park Failures and the Lessons They Leave Behind
articles.summary: A detailed retrospective examines some of the biggest and most expensive theme park failures in U.S. history, from Las Vegas mega-resorts to niche concept parks that never found an audience. The analysis serves as a cautionary framework for FEC investors and developers evaluating new concepts, particularly those targeting underserved demographics or untested markets. Understanding what went wrong at scale can help smaller FEC operators make smarter site selection, concept, and capital decisions.

articles.title: Kings Dominion Launches Military Appreciation Program for Summer Opening
articles.summary: Kings Dominion is offering free single-day admission to active and retired military members during its Memorial Day Weekend Military Days promotion, alongside year-round discounted admission for service members. Military appreciation promotions are a proven traffic-driver and community goodwill builder for FEC and amusement park operators. Operators looking for effective seasonal promotional templates should consider how similar programs could be adapted for their own venues.

articles.title: Hasbro Launches World's First Monopoly-Branded Steakhouse in Mexico
articles.summary: Hasbro and Timeless Brands have opened the world's first Monopoly Steakhouse in San Pedro Garza García, Mexico, extending the iconic board game IP into high-end licensed dining. The concept demonstrates the expanding universe of IP-driven experiential entertainment and dining, a trend with direct implications for FEC operators considering licensed concept partnerships. As IP-led experiences proliferate, FEC operators should evaluate how brand licensing could enhance their entertainment and F&B offerings.

articles.title: Staffing Remains the Top Challenge for Attraction and Museum Directors in 2025
articles.summary: A new survey identifies lack of qualified staff as the single biggest challenge facing museum and attraction directors this year, a finding that resonates deeply across the broader FEC and attractions industry. Labor shortages continue to constrain operating hours, guest service quality, and expansion plans at venues of all sizes. FEC operators should prioritize workforce development, retention incentives, and scheduling technology as strategic responses to this ongoing challenge.
